item.title: From Trade to Taiwan, What the Trump-Xi Summit Did (and Didn’t) Accomplish
item.description: On today’s Big Take podcast: President Trump’s summit with Xi Jinping offered good optics, but gave little indication of progress on major sticking points.

item.title: Fed Names Powell Chair Pro Tempore Until Warsh Sworn In
item.description: The Federal Reserve board on Friday named Jerome Powell chair pro tempore until his successor Kevin Warsh is officially sworn in, the central bank said in a statement.

item.title: Berkshire Boosts Alphabet, Exits Amazon in CEO’s First Quarter
item.description: Greg Abel spent his first months as chief executive officer of Berkshire Hathaway Inc. boosting the conglomerate’s holding in Alphabet Inc. while exiting its bet on Amazon.com.

item.title: The Global Bond Rout Catches Up With Wall Street’s Risk Rally
item.description: Wall Street’s hard-charging risk rally just ran into the bond market.

item.title: Argentina’s YPF Seeks Incentives for $25 Billion Oil Project
item.description: State-run Argentine energy giant YPF SA announced a $25 billion project to accelerate oil production in the country, marking the largest investment since President Javier Milei took office.

item.title: S&P Raises Nigeria’s Rating for First Time in 14 Years
item.description: S&P Global Ratings lifted Nigeria’s credit rating for the first time since 2012, citing higher oil prices and the country’s improved capacity to refine and export crude.

item.title: Hedge Funds Boost Bullish Copper Wagers to Five-Month High
item.description: Money managers increased their bullish bets on copper to the highest since December before prices traded on New York’s Comex climbed to a fresh record high.

item.title: Meet The 26-Year-Old Undercutting BlackRock and Goldman ETFs
item.description: Corgi is barking about buffers

item.title: GIC, Itausa Lead $1 Billion Injection in Aegea, Diluting Equipav
item.description: Aegea will receive as much as $1 billion in fresh capital from shareholders, led by Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC Pte and Itaúsa SA, as it prepares a potential bid for Minas Gerais’ state-owned utility Copasa, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Is Starting to See Heavy Job Losses in Roles Exposed to AI
item.description: Several US occupations expected to be impacted by artificial intelligence saw heavy job losses for a second year in 2025, led by customer service representatives and certain types of secretaries and salespeople.

item.title: SpaceX Said to Plan Public IPO Filing as Soon as Wednesday
item.description: SpaceX is seeking to file publicly for its long-awaited IPO as soon as Wednesday, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China Allows Exports for 425 US Beef Plants, Trade Group Says
item.description: China has renewed export licenses for 425 US beef plants, making way for a full resumption of trade for the protein between the two countries, according to the US Meat Export Federation.

item.title: Kraken Cuts 150 Workers After Deploying AI; IPO May Slip to 2027
item.description: Kraken, one of the world’s oldest cryptocurrency exchanges, has cut some staff to reduce costs and may not go public as soon as initially expected due to a sharp decline in digital-asset prices, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Russia Orders Euroclear to Pay €200 Billion to Central Bank
item.description: A Russian court ordered the Euroclear Ltd. clearinghouse to pay €200 billion ($233 billion) to the nation’s central bank, according to the Interfax news service, in a suit related to the European Union’s freeze of Russian assets.

item.title: Iran Oil Flows Most Likely Cut by Spill, TankerTrackers Says
item.description: A pause in Iranian oil shipments from Kharg Island, the nation’s main export hub, is most likely the result of an oil spill in the area, according to TankerTrackers.com, a firm that uses satellite imagery to monitor vessel movements.

item.title: Atari’s Tycoon CEO Is Aiming for a New Retro Revival
item.description: Wade Rosen, the 41-year-old son of a wealthy Minnesota family, is aiming to make Atari the best retro video-game company in the world

item.title: Silver Slumps to Close Out a Rollercoaster Week of Price Swings
item.description: Silver collapsed well below $80 an ounce at the end of a week of extreme price swings, bringing a fresh bout of volatility to a market that’s been one of this year’s most turbulent trades.

item.title: Los capitalistas de Cuba podrían ser la solución para la isla tras escasez de petróleo
item.description: Las empresas privadas que surgieron en la isla a pesar de años de persecución serán fundamentales para lo que venga, ahora que Trump presiona para poner fin a 67 años de régimen unipartidista.

item.title: Inflation Risk Gives Corporate Bonds the Edge Over Sovereigns
item.description: Credit investors enticed by high yields are buying up corporate bonds, shrugging off the lingering Middle East conflict and focusing instead on robust results from blue-chip businesses.

item.title: Ghana Eyes Investment-Grade Rating With New IMF Policy Support
item.description: Ghana has asked the International Monetary Fund for a policy support program to sustain economic reforms and build on recent gains as its $3 billion bailout nears an end.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees $200 Billion Hedging Flows Propping Up Euro
item.description: The euro could climb to levels last seen five years ago as a drop in hedging costs would drive flows of more than $200 billion and prop up the currency, according to Morgan Stanley.

item.title: We Can’t Just Ignore the Rise in Yields
item.description: The 30-year yield nears its highest level since 2007.

item.title: Bond Futures at Risk From Rapid Hedging Overhaul as Yields Climb
item.description: A global surge in yields is threatening to cause a disruption in the Treasury futures market — the principal tool for hedging US government bonds — as traders stand to overhaul their positions.

item.title: Russia’s Wartime Economy Shrinks for First Time Since 2023
item.description: Russia’s economy contracted in the first quarter for the first time since the start of 2023, a setback for President Vladimir Putin’s demand for continued growth as he pursues the war in Ukraine.

item.title: Peru’s Economy Expanded Faster Than Expected in March
item.description: Peru’s economy posted better-than-expected growth in March, as construction and commerce shook off a two-week domestic natural gas shortage that hindered output.

item.title: G-7 to Discuss Bond Selloff as Yields Hit Multi-Decade Highs
item.description: Group of Seven finance chiefs are set to discuss the selloff in government bonds that’s sending yields on some benchmark securities to their highest levels in decades, with at least one member viewing the move as temporary.

item.title: Turkey’s Kontrolmatik Defaults on 450 Million Liras Debt
item.description: Turkey’s Kontrolmatik Enerji ve Muhendislik defaulted on two separate lira bonds that matured on Friday, according to a statement by Turkey’s Central Securities Depository, marking a rare corporate debt default in the country’s local bond market.

item.title: Goldman Floats Risk Transfer Deal Tied to Private Market Loans
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is sounding out investors about a significant risk transfer tied to a portfolio of loans to private market funds.

item.title: Communist Cuba Is Leaning on Capitalists as Food and Oil Run Out
item.description: Private businesses that emerged on the island despite years of persecution will be key to whatever comes next as Trump pushes to end 67 years of one-party rule.

item.title: Revolut Offers All Staffers £1000 to Help Lure In New Business
item.description: Revolut Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Nik Storonsky is offering employees across the digital bank £1000 ($1,334) to woo more businesses to bank with Revolut, setting it as its highest priority to further boost the digital lender’s growth, valuation and revenue.
